summary: Large stage decompilation PR for Onett and Mute City. It replaced many `/// #` placeholders with typed C for Mute City car/spline/collision/light command logic and Onett building, traffic, awning, and generator behavior. It also corrected ground-variable layouts, function prototypes, and helper return types. There was no PR body or review discussion in the dump, so rationale is inferred from the diff only.

key_files.note: +1122/-75; implements Mute City setup, command script processing, spline tracking, collision-line updates, 30-car simulation, car effects, and light color save/restore.
+670/-56; implements Onett callbacks/stage data, awning physics, building state machine, car traffic state machines, item callbacks, sounds, and timed generators.
Types Mute City and Onett ground variable unions, adds GXColor support, splits Onett vars into awning/general, building, and car structs.
Replaces a raw 0x528-byte float-field blob with `grMc_CarEntry` plus `grMc_8049F4B8[30]` and a size assert.
Replaces several `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` prototypes with typed Mute City signatures.
Adds `it/forward.h` and typed prototypes for Onett item, sound, and awning functions.
Header signature for `grLib_801C9808` updated from `void` to `HSD_Generator*`.

what_changed: Changed `grLib_801C9808` from `void` to `HSD_Generator*`, returning the result of `hsd_8039EFAC`; callers now have the correct generator-producing signature.

Implemented Mute City `grMuteCity_801EFDF8` stage setup: starts animations, resolves car splines, caches camera/blast-zone offsets, creates related stage gobjs, registers mp joint callbacks, initializes material/light state, and hides a point light.

Implemented Mute City `grMuteCity_801F04B8` as a frame-command interpreter over `grMc_803E34E0`, handling delays, animations, mp joints, material swaps, camera/bounds/blast-zone updates, audio, and mode flags.

Added Mute City spline helpers and collision-line updates: `grMc_SplineEvalWrapped`, `grMuteCity_801F094C`, and `grMuteCity_801F0D20` evaluate wrapped splines around the 0.93/0.01 seam and update mp lines 0x31, 0x33, and 0x35.

Implemented Mute City car initialization and traffic logic via `grMuteCity_801F0F4C`, `grMuteCity_801F106C`, `grMuteCity_801F1328`, and `grMuteCity_801F1A34`, using 30 `grMc_CarEntry` records, sorted indices, speed/spacing flags, spline-based transforms, effects, item hazards, and sounds.

Converted the old Mute City static car storage from a single giant raw struct to `grMc_CarEntry grMc_8049F4B8[30]`; the 30 small age-update wrappers now index `[0]` through `[29]` instead of hard-coded offsets.

Implemented `grMuteCity_801F290C` to save and restore up to four light object colors using `saved_colors[4]` and `GXColor`, and to tint lights with `Ground.x6C` while a condition from `grLib_801C96E8` is false.
Fixed Mute City collision helper access in `grMuteCity_801F2C10` by treating `xE4` and `xF0` as `Vec3` values instead of scalar floats.
Expanded Onett `StageCallbacks` from two empty entries to six callback entries and added `StageData grOt_803E2858` for the stage.

Expanded Onett stage parameters from sparse padding into named awning physics fields (`max_velocity`, thresholds, damping, spring force/constant, max displacement) while retaining unknown `xNN` fields where semantics remain unclear.
Implemented Onett general setup `grOnett_801E3A34`, initializing awning jobjs, camera subject storage, generator timers, callbacks, collision helpers, and animation/ground updates.

Implemented Onett building behavior in `grOnett_801E3DA0` and updated `grOnett_801E40E4` to use named `onett_building` fields for state, next_state, hit_count, frame, and timer instead of unrelated/unknown union members.

Implemented Onett car setup and traffic state machines in `grOnett_801E41C8` and `grOnett_801E43E0`, including car jobj arrays, item proxies, randomized car choice, left-to-right and right-to-left states, fighter proximity checks, camera-position checks, item animation, warning/crash sounds, and hazard toggles.
Implemented Onett item/sound callbacks: `grOnett_801E5030` handles car item collision outcomes, and `grOnett_801E5194` maps car index plus sound type to SFX IDs.

Implemented Onett awning physics in `grOnett_801E5214`, applying a damped spring update to awning Y translation and triggering generator/sound effects with cooldown; `grOnett_801E54B4` now updates named awning tracking fields.
Implemented Onett timed generator logic in `grOnett_801E5538`, alternating between spawning a generator at a transformed JObj point via `grLib_801C96F8` and stopping it with `grLib_801C9834`.
smart_moves: Factoring Mute City car state into `grMc_CarEntry[30]` made a 30-record repeating layout explicit and removed hundreds of meaningless float fields from the static header.
Splitting Onett ground variables by role (`onett`, `onett_building`, `onettcar`) avoided misleading reuse of unrelated union members such as `kongo3` and `unk`.
Typing `Ground.x6C` and Mute City `saved_colors` as `GXColor` aligned the data layout with `HSD_LObjGetColor`, `HSD_LObjSetColor`, and the light tinting code.
Keeping semantic names only where the diff supports them, while retaining `xNN` names for uncertain stage parameters and fields, preserves evidence-grounded decomp progress.
The wrapped spline helper captures the special Mute City track seam behavior at constants 0.93, 0.01, and 0.07999999 instead of assuming a simple modulo spline.

Updating prototypes and includes alongside implementations reduced local externs and `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` use, especially for `grLib_801C9808`, `grMuteCity_801F094C`, `grMuteCity_801F1A0C`, and Onett item callbacks.
decomp_lessons: When a static blob repeats at a constant stride, introduce a typed element struct plus an array and `STATIC_ASSERT`; it can unlock cleaner indexing and many downstream matches.
Ground variable unions should be split by stage gobj role when callbacks reveal distinct layouts; Onett uses separate general/awning, building, and car data.
Use exact offset comments in `types.h` while migrating from unknown fields to typed fields; this keeps layout reviewable while improving readability.
Generator helpers may return useful handles even when older prototypes were `void`; check underlying HSD calls before freezing signatures.
Spline-based stage hazards can depend on hand-tuned seam interpolation constants; preserve literal constants and branch structure during decomp.
Lighting code should use concrete color structs, not integer placeholders, when interacting with LObj color APIs.
Callback signatures should be derived from the factory/registration site: Onett car material callbacks use `Item_GObj*` and `Ground*`, and Mute City material callbacks use `HSD_GObj*` and `Ground*`.
Replacing unknown union aliases with role-specific names helps future reviewers see when a function is building state, traffic state, awning physics, or camera subject code.
naming_conventions: Address-based function names remain in place (`grMuteCity_801F1A34`, `grOnett_801E43E0`) where no semantic rename is evidenced.
Stage-local prefixes follow existing convention: `grMc_` for Mute City data/helpers and `grOt_` for Onett data/helpers.
Unknown fields are retained as `xNN` or bit names such as `xD0_flags.b23` when semantics are not established by the diff.
New role-specific GroundVars names include `grOnett_AwningData`, `grOnett_GroundVars`, `grOnett_Building_GroundVars`, and `grOnett_Car_GroundVars`.
Semantically clear fields are named directly, such as `subject`, `gen`, `timer`, `awnings`, `car_jobjs`, `curr_car`, `state_a`, `timer_b`, `speed_b`, and `saved_colors`.
Helper names describe behavior without overclaiming original names: `grMc_SplineEvalWrapped` and `grOnett_SfxLookup`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Observed use of `PAD_STACK` in newly implemented functions preserves stack shape for matching-sensitive code.
Literal float constants were preserved: examples include `0.932775f`, `0.001f`, `0.0004f`, `0.93f`, `0.01f`, `0.07999999f`, `0.2617994f`, and `3.1415927f`.
Typed bitfields and exact integer widths (`s16`, `u16`, `u8`) were used for ground vars and car flags to keep offsets aligned.
The Mute City car-order routine uses an explicit sorted index array `grMc_8049F440[30]`, matching the observed traffic comparison pattern without moving car records.
Some source-level oddities, such as repeated spline wrap blocks in `grMuteCity_801F1A34` and explicit null-step patterns while walking LObjs, appear retained to preserve codegen.
Function headers were tightened from `UNK_RET`/`UNK_PARAMS` to concrete signatures only where call sites and implementations show the needed types.
Changing Mute City `xE4` and `xF0` from scalar-like fields to `Vec3` enabled natural `.x`/`.y` access and fixed slope calculations in `grMuteCity_801F2C10`.
review_feedback: No PR body, issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the slice; no human review feedback or reviewer intent can be extracted.
follow_up_queries: Search later PRs for semantic names for `grMc_CarEntry` fields `x0`, `x4`, `x8`, `xC`, `x20`, `x22_flags`, `x24`, and `x28`.
Find whether `grMc_803E34E0` command IDs 1-26 were later documented or replaced with an enum.
Check whether `grMuteCity_GroundVars.xD0_flags.b23` received a semantic name for the collision-line mode.
Search for later cleanup of duplicated spline seam interpolation in `grMuteCity_801F1A34`.
Look for follow-up PRs that rename Onett `state_a`, `state_b`, `x108`, `x110`, and `timer_b` in `grOnett_Car_GroundVars`.
Verify whether Onett stage parameter unknowns `x24` through `x64` were later named from data or behavior.
Search all call sites of `grLib_801C9808` to see whether any later code stores the returned `HSD_Generator*`.
